prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1775554744050,"sessionID":"ses_298b0f254ffetdeFlCqtvuaRJz","part":{"id":"prt_d674f2a32001tLhg56DeAwKE80","sessionID":"ses_298b0f254ffetdeFlCqtvuaRJz","messageID":"msg_d674f205c0017cg93oQy95hsCY","type":"tool","callID":"call_464524dcacc845e9aef25ca8","tool":"write","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments.
"""

import argparse
import sys
from typing import List


def generate_service_file(
    name: str,
    exec_path: str,
    user: str = "root",
    description: str = None,
    after: List[str] = None,
    restart: str = "on-failure",
    restart_sec: int = 5,
    output: str = None,
    wanted_by: str = "multi-user.target",
    env: List[str] = None,
    working_dir: str = None
) -> str:
    """Generate a systemd service file content."""
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"
    
    if after is None:
        after = ["network.target"]
    
    if output is None:
        output = f"{name}.service"
    
    # Validate restart option
    valid_restart = {"no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"}
    if restart not in valid_restart:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid restart option: {restart}. Must be one of {valid_restart}")
    
    # Generate the service file content
    service_file = f"""[Unit]
Description={description}
After={';'.join(after)}

[Service]
Type=simple
User={user}
ExecStart={exec_path}
Restart={restart}
RestartSec={restart_sec}
"""
    
    # Add WorkingDirectory if provided
    if working_dir:
        service_file += f"WorkingDirectory={working_dir}\n"
    
    # Add Environment lines if provided
    if env:
        for env_var in env:
            service_file += f"Environment={env_var}\n"
    
    # Add Install section
    service_file += f"""
[Install]
WantedBy={wanted_by}
"""
    
    return service_file


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate systemd service unit files")
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Service name")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable path")
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument("--description", help="Service description (default: {name} service)")
    parser.add_argument("--after", action="append", default=["network.target"], help="After dependency (can be specified multiple times, default: network.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart", choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"], 
                       default="on-failure", help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart-sec", type=int, default=5, help="Restart delay in seconds (default: 5)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output file name (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument("--wanted-by", default="multi-user.target", help="WantedBy target (default: multi-user.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", help="Environment variable (KEY=VALUE format, can be specified multiple times)")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory")
    
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    try:
        service_content = generate_service_file(
            name=args.name,
            exec_path=args.exec,
            user=args.user,
            description=args.description,
            after=args.after,
            restart=args.restart,
            restart_sec=args.restart_sec,
            output=args.output,
            wanted_by=args.wanted_by,
            env=args.env,
            working_dir=args.working_dir
        )
        
        if args.output:
            with open(args.output, "w") as f:
                f.write(service_content)
            print(f"Service file written to {args.output}")
        else:
            print(service_content)
            
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":

    user: str = "root",
    description: str = None,
    after: List[str] = None,

    wanted_by: str = "multi-user.target",
    env: List[str] = None,
    working_dir: str = None

    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"

        after = ["network.target"]
    
    if output is None:
        output = f"{name}.service"
    
    # Validate restart option
    valid_restart = {"no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"}
    if restart not in valid_restart:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid restart option: {restart}. Must be one of {valid_restart}")
    
    # Generate the service file content
    service_file = f"""[Unit]
Description={description}
After={';'.join(after)}

[Service]
Type=simple

Restart={restart}

    parser.add_argument("--restart", choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"],

[Service]
Type=simple

ExecStart={exec_path}

"""

        service_file += f"WorkingDirectory={working_dir}\n"

            service_file += f"Environment={env_var}\n"
    
    # Add Install section
    service_file += f"""
[Install]
WantedBy={wanted_by}
"""
    
    return service_file


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate systemd service unit files")
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Service name")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable path")
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument("--description", help="Service description (default: {name} service)")
    parser.add_argument("--after", action="append", default=["network.target"], help="After dependency (can be specified multiple times, default: network.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart", choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"], 
                       default="on-failure", help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart-sec", type=int, default=5, help="Restart delay in seconds (default: 5)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output file name (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument("--wanted-by", default="multi-user.target", help="WantedBy target (default: multi-user.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", help="Environment variable (KEY=VALUE format, can be specified multiple times)")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory")
    
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    # Handle the after argument properly - if user provided --after, it should not include the default
    if args.after and args.after == ["network.target"]:
        # This means no --after was provided, so we use default
        after = ["network.target"]
    elif args.after:
        # User provided --after arguments, use those
        after = args.after
    else:
        # Fallback
        after = ["network.target"]
    
    try:
        service_content = generate_service_file(
            name=args.name,
            exec_path=args.exec,
            user=args.user,
            description=args.description,
            after=after,
            restart=args.restart,

            print(f"Service file written to {args.output}")
        else:
            print(service_content)
            
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"
}

The script includes:

files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments.
"""

import argparse
import sys
from typing import List, Optional


def generate_service_file(
    name: str,
    exec_path: str,
    user: str = "root",
    description: Optional[str] = None,
    after: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    restart: str = "on-failure",
    restart_sec: int = 5,
    output: Optional[str] = None,
    wanted_by: str = "multi-user.target",
    env: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    working_dir: Optional[str] = None,
) -> str:
    """Generate a systemd service file content."""
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"

    # Use default if after is None or empty
    if after is None or len(after) == 0:
        after = ["network.target"]

    if output is None:
        output = f"{name}.service"

    # Validate restart option
    valid_restart = {
        "no",
        "on-success",
        "on-failure",
        "on-abnormal",
        "on-watchdog",
        "on-abort",
        "always",
    }
    if restart not in valid_restart:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Invalid restart option: {restart}. Must be one of {valid_restart}"
        )

    # Generate the service file content
    service_file = f"""[Unit]
Description={description}
After={";".join(after)}

[Service]
Type=simple
User={user}
ExecStart={exec_path}
Restart={restart}
RestartSec={restart_sec}
"""

    # Add WorkingDirectory if provided
    if working_dir:
        service_file += f"WorkingDirectory={working_dir}\n"

    # Add Environment lines if provided
    if env:
        for env_var in env:
            service_file += f"Environment={env_var}\n"

    # Add Install section
    service_file += f"""
[Install]
WantedBy={wanted_by}
"""

    return service_file


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate systemd service unit files")
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Service name")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable path")
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description", help="Service description (default: {name} service)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        help="After dependency (can be specified multiple times, default: network.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        choices=[
            "no",
            "on-success",
            "on-failure",
            "on-abnormal",
            "on-watchdog",
            "on-abort",
            "always",
        ],
        default="on-failure",
        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec",
        type=int,
        default=5,
        help="Restart delay in seconds (default: 5)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output file name (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="WantedBy target (default: multi-user.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        help="Environment variable (KEY=VALUE format, can be specified multiple times)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Handle the after argument properly
    # If --after is not provided, use default; if provided, use the provided values
    after = args.after if args.after is not None else ["network.target"]

    try:
        service_content = generate_service_file(
            name=args.name,
            exec_path=args.exec,
            user=args.user,
            description=args.description,
            after=after,
            restart=args.restart,
            restart_sec=args.restart_sec,
            output=args.output,
            wanted_by=args.wanted_by,
            env=args.env,
            working_dir=args.working_dir,
        )

        if args.output:
            with open(args.output, "w") as f:
                f.write(service_content)
            print(f"Service file written to {args.output}")
        else:
            print(service_content)

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

[Unit]
Description=My Application
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=www-data
ExecStart=/usr/bin/myapp
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

[Unit]
Description=myapp service
After=network.target;systemd-resolved

[Service]
Type=simple
User=root
ExecStart=/usr/bin/myapp
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
